label: short
chain_of_thought:
Okay

Let me compute each floor function:

1. $\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{7} \right\rfloor$
2. $\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{11} \right\rfloor$
3. $\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{13} \right\rfloor$

First, I'll do each division and then take the floor (i.e., the greatest integer less than or equal to the value).

1. $ \frac{1000}{7} \approx 142.857...$, so $\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{7} \right\rfloor = 142$
2. $ \frac{1000}{11} \approx 90.909...$, so $\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{11} \right\rfloor = 90$
3. $ \frac{1000}{13} \approx 76.923...$, so $\left\lfloor \frac{1000}{13} \right\rfloor = 76$

Now let me add them together:

$142 + 90 + 76 = 308$

So the answer is 308.
